description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player with a rectangular base featuring a wood-textured upper frame and a metallic lower section. The top surface is dominated by a large, circular turntable platter with concentric grooves, centrally positioned and surrounded by a black surface. A tonearm, colored in gray and black, rests diagonally across the platter, with a visible pivot and stylus area. The control panel includes two rectangular buttons or sliders and a square button, all located on the top surface near the platter. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible dust cover, and the materials suggest a combination of wood, metal, and plastic. The object appears sturdy and functional, suitable for placement in urban environments such as cafes, garages, or music shops.
description: A vintage record player with a wood-framed base, large black platter, and visible tonearm, featuring simple control buttons on the top surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the record player, the circular platter, tonearm resting on the right, and two control panels on the left and right.
description_view_1: Angled view from the front-left, highlighting the wood texture, the tonearm's length, and the control buttons' positions.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back edge of the record player, the platter, and the tonearm now on the left side.
description_view_3: Angled view from the front-right, emphasizing the square button and the tonearm's pivot mechanism.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and centrally located, the platter is large and circular with visible concentric grooves, occupying most of the top surface. It appears to be made of black plastic or metal, with a matte finish and a small central spindle. No additional markings or designs are visible.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending diagonally from the upper left toward the center of the platter. It is straight, medium-length, and appears to be made of gray plastic or metal with a matte finish. The tonearm is resting on its support, with a visible pivot mechanism.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is present, attached to the end of the tonearm. It is small, pointed, and appears as a distinct component, likely made of metal or plastic.
attribute_control_knobs: There are three visible control elements: two rectangular sliders or buttons and one square button, all located on the top surface near the platter. They are gray or silver in color, made of plastic or metal, and have a simple, unmarked design.
